name: Kaaawa
desc: Tiny windward coast village hugging the cliffs — dramatic Ko'olau backdrop, Crouching Lion, very remote and peaceful.
insider: The drive along Kamehameha Highway through Kaaawa is the most scenic stretch on Oahu — slow down and pull over often
Crouching Lion trailhead is unmarked and the trail is steep — ask a local or look for the pulloff near the old inn
Kaaawa Beach Park is a quiet local beach with mountain-meets-ocean scenery that feels like a movie set
The community is tiny — maybe 1,300 residents — and very welcoming to visitors who show respect
Kualoa Ranch across the highway offers ATV tours, horseback riding, and film location tours — Jurassic Park was shot here
There is essentially one restaurant and one store — stock up in Kaneohe before heading this way
The Ko'olau cliff walls that rise directly behind the village are some of the most dramatic scenery in all of Hawaii
Swanzy Beach Park just south of town has better swimming conditions and more shade than the main beach park
The area floods during heavy rains with runoff from the cliffs — avoid camping in low areas during storm warnings
Ancient Hawaiians considered this valley sacred — the entire area carries deep cultural and spiritual significance
